songs.title: P-P-Pop
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
P! P! P-p-pop!
(p… p… p…)

[Hook]
P! P! P-p-p!
What begins with P?
P-p-pig! P-p-pig!
It's a pig! (a pig!)

[Verse 2]
P! P! P-p-p!
What begins with P?
P-p-pen! P-p-pen!
It's a pen! (a pen!)

[Verse 3]
P! P! P-p-p!
What begins with P?
P-p-panda! P-p-panda!
It's a panda! (a panda!)

[Verse 4]
P! P! P-p-p!
What begins with P?
P-p-penguin! P-p-penguin!
It's a penguin! (a penguin!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (YES?!)
P-p-potato! That IS P!
(but the potato is NOT the answer!)
(p… p… p…)

[Outro]
P-p-pig! P-p-pig!
It's a pig! (a pig!)
P! P! P-p-pop!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The P sound is a quiet lip-pop /p/ — a puff of air with no voice, NEVER 'pee' (letter name) and NEVER 'puh' (schwa). The 'P-p-pop!' chant is crisp air-puffs on the beat. RUNNING-GAG TWIST: potato really DOES start with P this week, so the class shouts 'YES?!' — but it's still not the answer, so the joke flips into a giggle. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: The Cat Naps
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
(nap… nap… the cat naps…)

[Hook]
The cat naps! The cat naps!
Where does the cat nap?
The cat naps on the mat! (the mat!)
The cat naps on the mat!

[Verse 2]
The cat naps! The cat naps!
Where does the cat nap?
The cat naps on the map! (a map?!)
The cat naps on the map!

[Verse 3]
The cat naps! The cat naps!
Where does the cat nap?
The cat naps on the cap! (a cap?!)
The cat naps on the cap!

[Bridge]
Pat the cat. (pat, pat!)
Tap, tap, tap!
The cat can nap!

[Outro]
The cat naps on the pan.
(shhh… the cat is asleep…)
The cat naps on a POTATO!
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'The cat naps on the ___.' — the nap-anywhere cat. A single decodable word swaps per verse (mat, map, cap, pan). The bridge introduces 'pat' and 'tap' as the wake-up actions. Whisper the outro, then the potato hits loud.

spreads.artNote: The tabby cat fast asleep, curled in a warm spotlight — the sleepiest cat in the world. Sets up the running joke: this cat naps EVERYWHERE.

spreads.text: The cat naps on the mat.
spreads.artNote: Cat asleep, sprawled across the familiar woven mat, utterly relaxed.

spreads.text: The cat naps on the map!
spreads.artNote: Cat asleep on top of an open paper map, one paw over a whole continent. Absurd.

spreads.text: The cat naps on the cap!
spreads.artNote: Cat curled snugly on top of a baseball cap like it's a little bed, only ears and tail showing.

spreads.text: The cat naps on the pan!
spreads.artNote: Cat asleep coiled over a frying pan on the counter — peak ridiculous napping.
A gentle child's hand patting the sleeping cat's head — soft, tender. Teaching 'pat'.
A finger tapping near the cat, tiny sound lines — trying politely to wake it. The cat does not stir.
The cat sleeps blissfully through everything, Sam, Segina and the ant tiptoeing around it, giving up. The cat wins.
The cat asleep, using the potato as a pillow. The gag noun is left blank — the class SHOUTS 'POTATO' from the picture and howls. Deadpan potato pillow.

sentences: The cat naps on the mat.
The cat naps on the map.
The cat naps on the cap.
Pat the cat. Tap, tap, tap.
extras: Moveable alphabet: build p-a-t, n-a-p, m-a-p, t-a-p, c-a-p, p-a-n
Sandpaper letter 'p' + sand tray — the quiet lip-pop /p/
'Pat the cat' gentleness ritual — practise a soft pat on a plush cat
Nap-anywhere hide game: hide the sleeping-cat card in silly places, class reads 'The cat naps on the ___.'

teacherFocus: Teach the quiet lip-pop /p/ — a puff of air, never 'puh'. This is the week the potato finally starts with the target sound, so the sound song's running gag flips (the class shouts 'YES?!') — lean into the giggle, it is initial-sound discrimination disguised as comedy. Blend p-a-t, n-a-p, m-a-p, t-a-p and live the nap-anywhere cat all week: hide the sleeping-cat card in silly places and read 'The cat naps on the ___.' Practise a gentle 'pat' on a plush cat as a real gentleness lesson.
